title: Bounded Worker Pool, Origin Fairness, Cancellation, and Shutdown · Language Lens: Feed Go Concurrency Budget
summary: The scheduler launches one goroutine per source, results block after cancellation, one origin occupies all workers, panics leak claims, and shutdown exits before commits settle. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go concurrency budget increment in bounded origin-aware ingestion worker pool. Learners progress through prediction, expl…
objectives: Derive global, tenant, origin, database, memory, and queue budgets and enforce the minimum active constraint.
Test crash after claim, expiry during fetch, duplicate claim, heartbeat loss, database failover, cancellation, and stale completion.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Concurrency Budget · Theory Feed Go Concurrency Budget
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 3548 during a cross-tenant privacy audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove bounded origin-aware worker ownership, failure isolation, cancellation, and joined shutdown; untrusted XML and feed content must not cross admission or rendering boundaries; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Derive global, tenant, origin, database, memory, and queue budgets and enforce the minimum active constraint.
